question: Write a C++ program with functions for array operations: 1) InputArray to get array elements from user, 2) DisplayArray to show array elements, 3) FindMax to return maximum element, 4) FindMin to return minimum element, 5) CalculateSum to return sum of all elements. Use these functions to process an array of integers.

test_cases.expected: Enter numbers (enter -1 to stop): You entered: 1 2 3 4 5
Enter numbers (enter -1 to stop): You entered: 10 20

errors.description: No input validation: Program doesn't check if cin operation succeeds, which could cause undefined behavior with invalid input.
No memory allocation check: Program doesn't verify if 'new' operation succeeded before using the pointer.
No bounds checking: Program stores input without verifying array bounds, potentially causing buffer overflow.
No null pointer check: Program doesn't verify if arr is null before deallocation or dereferencing.
